title: Crunchy Indian Beet Curry
description: A hearty and earthy Indian curry featuring the vibrant color of beets, with a delightful crunch from toasted nuts and seeds, perfect for a cozy evening meal. This dish is paleo-friendly and free from common allergens.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and cook until they are translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
Add the diced beets, ground cumin, curry powder, and salt. Cook for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beets start to soften.
Add a few drops of red food coloring, if using, to enhance the color of the dish.
Reduce heat to low and let the curry simmer, covered, for about 20 minutes, or until the beets are tender.
While the curry is cooking, toast the sliced almonds in a small pan over low heat, stirring frequently, until fragrant and lightly browned.
To serve, garnish the curry with toasted almonds and chopped fresh cilantro.
Serve warm over cauliflower rice or with paleo-friendly naan bread.
